extraction.one_line_summary: BENNETT Sam won the sprint after VAN POPPEL Danny delivered a superior leadout in the final 200 meters, beating PETERSEN Mads and MØRKØV Michael.
tactical_patterns: VAN POPPEL Danny's 200-meter leadout proved decisive over Trek's earlier positioning for PETERSEN Mads at the finish.
Jumbo-Visma controlled entire stage despite no sprint interest, protecting TEUNISSEN Mike's red jersey through flat team control.
MØRKØV Michael found BENNETT Sam's wheel but lacked acceleration to come around despite Israel's earlier workload.

rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: first win since difficult multi-year period, now has stage win in every Grand Tour since 2018
rider_signals.VAN POPPEL Danny: positive: delivered race-winning leadout from 200m showing elite domestique value for sprint trains
rider_signals.PETERSEN Mads: neutral: Trek positioning adequate but lost to superior Bora leadout execution in final 200 meters
rider_signals.MØRKØV Michael: negative: found correct wheel but couldn't convert despite Deceuninck carrying heavy workload throughout stage
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: negative: only fifth place despite expectations, suggests form concerns early in Vuelta
rider_signals.GROVES Kaden: negative: tenth place indicates positioning or form problems in straightforward sprint finish
